repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem

`nvim_get_option_value()` supports window-local (`win`) and buffer-local (`buf`) context via `Dict(option)`, but there is **no way to query tabpage-local options**. In particular, **`cmdheight` can be set per-tabpage**, yet the API either errors (unknown key) or always returns the global/current value, making it impossible for plugins/UIs to reliably read tab-local `cmdheight`.

Fixes #31140

## Solution

- Extend `Dict(option)` to accept a **`tab`** key (Tabpage handle).
- Teach `nvim_get_option_value()` to **resolve `cmdheight` in the specified tabpage**, returning the tab-local value when present.
- Refactored arguments in `validate_option_value_args` so `tab` is **mutually exclusive** with `win`/`buf`/etc., and **only supported for `cmdheight`** for now (other options continue to behave as before).
- Add a functional test covering reading tab-local `cmdheight` via `nvim_get_option_value()` with `{ tab = ... }`.

AI-assisted: Cursor

comment_data.body: Even if it's uncommon, I think we do need this "shape" in place,  👍 . Similar for `event-args` https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/38537 .
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -3539,6 +3538,9 @@ nvim_get_option_value({name}, {opts})                *nvim_get_option_value()*
                 • win: |window-ID|. Used for getting window local options.
                 • buf: Buffer number. Used for getting buffer local options.
                   Implies {scope} is "local".
+                • tab: |tab-ID| for tab-local options. Currently only supports
+                  "cmdheight". Cannot be used with "scope", "win", "buf", or
+                  "filetype". Tabpage `0` means the current tabpage.

comment_data.body:
This should have been using `VALIDATE_CON`, but I guess I missed this when I introduced `VALIDATE_CON`.

Please ensure that the code you touched in this PR uses VALIDATE_CON where appropriate.

don't need to mention this. in the rare case that users might make that mistake, it's caught by a clear validation error.
```suggestion
                  "cmdheight".
```

                 • scope: One of "global" or "local". Analogous to |:setglobal|
                   and |:setlocal|, respectively.
+                • tab: |tab-ID| for tab-local options. Currently only supports
+                  "cmdheight". Cannot be used with "scope", "win", "buf", or

comment_data.body: please put the validation tests in the 'validation' block (line 1893)

+      eq(4, api.nvim_get_option_value('cmdheight', { tab = tab2 }))

+      eq(
+        "Conflict: 'tab' not allowed with 'win'",

comment_data.body: is this a TODO? please add a TODO comment if so. and this seems like VALIDATE_CON applies.

this could be one VALIDATE_CON call. 

    VALIDATE_CON(<condition>, "filetype", "'scope', 'buf' or 'win'", ...

Switched to VALIDATE_CON. On the TODO question: as I understand it, `nvim_get_option_info2` returns metadata, and none of those fields are tab-local for any option, so tab has no behavior to attach to it. I kept the rejection so that tab stays a `nvim_get_option_value`-only feature for now. I added a VALIDATE_CON rejection and matching TODO in `nvim_set_option_value` because tab-local setting is plausible future work. Happy to revisit this area if you'd rather have a TODO marker anyway.

> On the TODO question: as I understand it, `nvim_get_option_info2` returns metadata, and none of those fields are tab-local for any option, so tab has no behavior to attach to it

I would expect `nvim_get_option_info2` (and `set_...`) to support tab-local 'cmdheight' if `nvim_get_option_value` supports it...

comment_regular_data.body: Hopefully this commit resolves the rest your comments @justinmk. I appreciate your patience, as I'm a longtime user of Neovim, but this is the first time I've ever contributed to an open-source project and I found it quite daunting. My work was part of an assignment for a software engineering course, and even though I just graduated, I'd still like to make sure my contributions are useful to the rest of the nvim community. Thanks for your help in reviewing :)

title: fix(api): nvim_get_option_value tab-local 'cmdheight'
